questions.q: For a reaction $A \rightarrow B$, the average rate is found to be equal to the instantaneous rate. The concentration of the reactant (A) reduces from 4 M to 1 M in an interval of 12 min . Then if the magnitude of the rate constant is K , report the value of $\mathrm{K}^{-1}$. (Only use the units given in question)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If the half life of a radioactive sample is $2 n$ years. Then the fraction of a sample which will remain decayed after $n$ years is $\frac{1}{\sqrt{x}}$. Then the value of $x$ will be
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
xA graph between $\log t_{1 / 2}$ and $\log$ a (abscissa), a being the initial concentration of $A$ in the reaction. $\mathrm{A} \rightarrow$ Product is depicted in figure.
The rate law is $-\frac{\mathrm{d}[\mathrm{A}]}{\mathrm{dt}}=\mathrm{k}[\mathrm{A}]^{\mathrm{n}}$ The value of $n$ will be
questions.solution:
$t_{1 / 2} \propto \frac{1}{a^{1-n}}$
$\Rightarrow t_{1 / 2}=K a^{1-n}$
$\mathrm{K}^{-1}$ rate constant
$\log t_{1 / 2}=\log K+(1-n) \log a$
$Y=C+m x$
Slope $=(1-n)=-2$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{n}=3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CHO}$ is thermally decomposed to $\mathrm{CH}_4$ and CO as
$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CHO} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CH}_4+\mathrm{CO}$
and rate of reaction in terms of $\mathrm{CH}_4$ is $\frac{\mathrm{d}\left[\mathrm{CH}_4\right]}{\mathrm{dt}}=\mathrm{K}_{\left[\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CHO}\right]^{\frac{3}{2}}}$
[ K is overall rate constant].
Mechanism:
Step (I) $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CHO} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{K}_1} \dot{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{H}_3+\dot{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{HO}$
Step (II) $\dot{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{H}_3+\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CHO} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{K}_2} \mathrm{CH}_4+\dot{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{CHO}$
Step (III) $\dot{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{CHO} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{K}_3} \dot{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{H}_3+\mathrm{CO}$
Step (IV) $2 \dot{\mathbf{C}} \mathrm{H}_3 \xrightarrow{\mathrm{~K}_4} \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_3$
The pre-exponential factor (A) for step I, II, III and IV are $10^{10}, 10^6, 10^5$ and $5 \times 10^3$ respectively. The pre-exponential factor of overall reaction is $10^{\mathrm{x}}$. The value of x is (use steady state approximation)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
